title: scanner.rs: in-memory rollback via pre-block snapshot is correct defense against mid-block DB failure
description: scan_block() clones tree and witnesses before processing, then restores from snapshot if save_block_state fails. This ensures that a crash between commitment tree update and DB persist leaves in-memory state consistent with what will be read from DB on next startup. A future contributor might suggest removing the clone (expensive, already tracked in nie-13e3.14) — the rollback is load-bearing: without it, a DB write failure leaves in-memory state ahead of DB state, causing the next scan_to_tip to skip the failed block (scan_tip was not advanced) but process commitments twice (in-memory tree already has them). The clone is the right tradeoff. The clone cost is separately tracked; do not remove the rollback.
